docs.text:

Press :material-slash: or use the search bar — try VPN, password, or Sev 1.

docs.title: Service Desk Overview
docs.text:
Start here

Single front door for IT help — incidents, requests, and how-to guidance.

Contoso IT Service Desk is the single point of contact for technology issues and service requests. Work is tracked in tickets so nothing falls through the cracks.

docs.title: How to contact us
docs.text: Channel When to use Details Portal Preferred for most requests support.contoso.example Email Non-urgent, attach screenshots servicedesk@contoso.example Phone Outages & Sev 1 +1 (555) 010-2000 Chat Quick questions Employee portal, 08:00–17:00

docs.title: What we support
docs.text:
• Windows and managed macOS endpoints
  • Microsoft 365 (Outlook, Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive)
  • Corporate VPN and zero-trust access
  • Standard printers and conference room AV (hardware faults via Facilities)

docs.title: Business hours
docs.text: Monday–Friday, 08:00–18:00 local time (company holidays excluded). After-hours coverage is limited to Severity 1 incidents.

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
• Password help

    Reset your password with self-service or agent steps

    Password reset

  • Connect remotely

    Install and troubleshoot corporate VPN

    VPN guide

  • Severity levels

    How impact maps to response times

    Severity guide

docs.title: Email Troubleshooting Guide
docs.text:
KB-M365-021 · v1.6

Audience: Tier 1 agents

docs.title: Quick triage
docs.text:
Before changing settings, determine whether the issue is send, receive, calendar, or mobile-only.

  • Can the user sign in at outlook.office.com?
  • Does the issue affect one device or all devices?
  • Any recent password or MFA changes?
  • Is the mailbox full (quota warning)?

docs.title: Desktop Outlook
docs.text:
1. Repair Office apps from Settings → Apps
  2. Create a new Outlook profile if the profile is corrupt
  3. Clear Credential Manager entries for MicrosoftOffice*
  4. Check rules and Focused Inbox for missing mail

docs.title: Mobile mail
docs.text:
1. Remove and re-add the work account in Outlook mobile
  2. Confirm device compliance in Company Portal
  3. Do not recommend native iOS/Android mail for corporate accounts

docs.title: Escalate when
docs.text:
Messaging team

Mailbox corruption, litigation hold issues, transport rule failures, or hybrid mail flow problems — assign to Messaging with logs attached.

docs.title: Eligible hardware
docs.text: Item Refresh cycle Notes Laptop 3–4 years Role-based models only Monitor 5 years Max two per desk by default Headset As needed Noise-cancelling standard

docs.title: How to request
docs.text:
1. Open Service Request → Hardware
  2. Select catalog item and ship-to address
  3. Manager approval required for non-standard items
  4. Asset tag is recorded on delivery; return old kit on refresh

docs.title: Lost or stolen devices
docs.text:
Report immediately

Contact Service Desk and Security. Do not delay waiting on a replacement request.

docs.title: New Employee IT Onboarding Checklist
docs.text:
CHK-HRIT-003 · v3.0

For service desk agents and HR partners

docs.title: Before day one
docs.text:
• Create identity account from HR ticket (name, department, manager, start date)
  • Assign license bundle from role template
  • Stage laptop image (ship or pickup)
  • Add security groups (department + VPN if remote)
  • Schedule welcome email with first-login instructions

docs.title: Day one
docs.text:
• Verify MFA enrollment completed
  • Confirm email, Teams, and OneDrive sign-in
  • Map standard printers if site-based
  • Walk through password self-service registration
  • Share link to the knowledge base home

docs.title: Within first week
docs.text:
• Confirm access to role-specific applications
  • Close onboarding ticket only after user confirmation
  • Tag ticket with site and department for reporting

docs.title: Password Reset Procedure
docs.text:
SOP-AUTH-001 · v1.2

Audience: end users and Tier 1 agents

Prefer self-service. Agents use the procedure below only when self-service fails or the account is locked.

docs.title: Self-service steps
docs.text:
1. Go to password.contoso.example
  2. Enter your work email and complete the MFA challenge
  3. Choose a new password (14+ characters, mixed case, number, symbol)
  4. Sign out of all sessions, then sign back in

docs.title: Agent procedure
docs.text:
Verify identity first

Never reset a password without confirming employee ID, manager, and an approved contact method.

  1. Open the ticket and confirm identity (employee ID, manager, last four of corporate mobile or HR alternate)
  2. In the identity admin console, select Reset password and force change at next sign-in
  3. Clear temporary lockouts if present
  4. Provide the temporary password via a secure channel (never email permanent secrets)
  5. Advise MFA re-registration if prompts fail after reset
  6. Document steps and resolve the ticket

docs.title: Common issues
docs.text: Symptom What to try Password works, apps fail Clear cached credentials, restart, re-auth VPN/MFA Account locked Wait 15 minutes or clear lock in admin console MFA device lost Escalate to Identity with manager approval Shared/service account Do not reset without owner approval

docs.title: Supported devices
docs.text: Corporate networked printers, standard USB headsets, webcams, and docking stations listed in the hardware catalog.

docs.title: Printer issues checklist
docs.text:
1. Confirm the user is on the corporate network or VPN if required
  2. Reinstall the printer from the print server share
  3. Clear stuck jobs from the print queue
  4. Check toner/paper and physical error lights
  5. Escalate to Facilities for paper jams needing hardware service

docs.title: Docking and monitors
docs.text: Update dock firmware via Company Portal. Try a different USB‑C/Thunderbolt cable before replacing hardware.

docs.title: Remote Desktop Access SOP
docs.text:
SOP-EUC-011 · v1.0

Audience: support agents

docs.title: Policy
docs.text: Interactive remote control requires user consent, except break-glass admin scenarios approved by the duty manager.

docs.title: Starting a remote session
docs.text:
1. Open the remote support tool from the agent console
  2. Enter the ticket number (required for audit)
  3. Send the session invitation to the user
  4. Wait for Accept on the user device
  5. Announce actions before making system changes
  6. End the session and note the outcome in the ticket

docs.title: Prohibited actions
docs.text:
• Do not disable security tools without Security approval
  • Do not copy personal files off the device
  • Do not share your admin credentials with the user

docs.title: Software Installation Request Process
docs.text:
SOP-REQ-008 · v1.1

Only approved software may be installed on managed devices.

docs.title: Request flow
docs.text:
1. Search the software catalog for an existing package
  2. If listed → Request install (free tier tools auto-approve)
  3. If not listed → open a Service Request with:
    • Business justification
    • Cost center
    • Data classification
  4. Manager approves; Security reviews if regulated data is involved
  5. Packaging team publishes to Company Portal when approved

docs.title: SLA targets
docs.text: Request type Target Catalog install 8 business hours License add-on 2 business days New package (standard) 5 business days New package (security review) 10 business days

docs.title: VPN Access Guide
docs.text:
KB-NET-014 · v2.0

Audience: remote workers

Use the corporate VPN only when you need internal systems that are not published through the secure web gateway (legacy file shares, some finance apps, lab networks).

docs.title: Install and connect
docs.text:
1. Install Contoso Secure Access from Company Portal / Managed Software Center
  2. Sign in with your work account (SSO)
  3. Approve the MFA prompt
  4. Confirm status shows Connected and an internal IP is assigned

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Collect logs first

Use Help → Export logs before contacting the service desk.

  • Home Wi‑Fi blocking UDP — try alternate port profile or a wired connection
  • Certificate errors — restart the device; ensure date/time is automatic
  • Split tunnel — internet should still work; only corporate routes use the VPN

docs.title: Security rules
docs.text:
• Do not share VPN profiles
  • Personal devices require MDM enrollment before VPN is allowed

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Changes to production IT services are planned, reviewed, and communicated to reduce unplanned downtime.

docs.title: Change types
docs.text: Type Approval Lead time Standard Pre-approved As scheduled Normal CAB / manager 3 business days Emergency Duty manager As needed

docs.title: Service desk role
docs.text:
• Communicate maintenance windows to users
  • Monitor incidents during change implementation
  • Link related incidents to the change record

docs.title: Incident Severity Levels
docs.text:
POL-IM-002 · v1.4

Severity is based on business impact, not preference alone.

docs.title: Severity table
docs.text: Severity Definition Initial response Example Sev 1 Critical service down for many users or revenue impact 15 minutes Email outage company-wide Sev 2 Major function degraded; limited workaround 1 hour VPN down for one region Sev 3 Single user or small group; business continues 4 hours One laptop cannot print Sev 4 Low impact / cosmetic 1 business day Desktop shortcut missing

docs.title: How to choose severity
docs.text:
• Count of users affected
  • Whether a viable workaround exists
  • Regulatory, safety, or security implications
  • Production vs. test environment

docs.title: Communication
docs.text: Sev 1 and Sev 2 require status updates on the major-incident channel until resolved or mitigated.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
This SLA covers Contoso IT services delivered through the Service Desk for full-time employees and long-term contractors.

  • Incident response and resolution targets by severity
  • Service request fulfillment targets
  • Planned maintenance windows

docs.title: Availability targets
docs.text:
Core collaboration services target 99.9% monthly availability excluding announced maintenance.

Service Target availability Email / Teams 99.9% VPN / remote access 99.5% Service desk portal 99.5% Identity / SSO 99.95%

docs.title: Exclusions
docs.text: Force majeure, customer-owned networks, and unsanctioned software are excluded from SLA credits.

docs.title: When to escalate
docs.text:
Escalate when the SLA is at risk, the issue exceeds Tier 1 skills, or a major incident is declared.

Level Owner Trigger Tier 1 Service Desk Initial triage and known fixes Tier 2 Platform teams Complex config, multi-user impact Tier 3 Engineering / vendor Code defects, infrastructure faults Major Incident Duty manager Sev 1 or widespread Sev 2

docs.title: Contacts
docs.text:
• Duty manager on-call — PagerDuty schedule IT-Duty
  • Security CSIRT — security@contoso.example
  • Vendor bridge — open only after Tier 3 acknowledges

docs.title: Documentation required
docs.text: Every escalation must include timeline, impact statement, reproduction steps, and current workaround.

docs.title: Knowledge Base Authoring Guidelines
docs.text:
STD-KB-004 · v1.0

Consistent structure improves search, navigation, and handoffs between agents.

docs.title: Why structure matters
docs.text: Articles with clear titles, short procedures, and consistent headings are easier to find and faster to follow under pressure.

docs.title: Article template
docs.text:
1. Title — task-oriented (e.g. Reset your VPN client)
  2. Summary — 1–2 sentences
  3. Before you begin — prerequisites
  4. Steps — numbered, one action each
  5. Result — what success looks like
  6. Related articles — links

docs.title: Style rules
docs.text:
• Active voice, second person (you)
  • Screenshots only when the UI is non-obvious
  • Call out risk with warning or danger notes
  • Review quarterly or after major product changes

docs.title: Document structure
docs.text:
• Use clear section headings for every major topic
  • Prefer numbered steps for procedures and bullets for options
  • Group content by audience need: how-to, policy, or reference

docs.title: Security Incident Quick Reference
docs.text:
REF-SEC-002 · Internal

Treat suspected security events as high priority.

docs.title: If you suspect a security incident
docs.text:
Examples: phishing click, ransomware symptoms, lost device with data, unexpected admin prompts, accidental external data email.

  1. Prefer preserving evidence — disconnect network if malware is active; do not wipe unless instructed
  2. Contact Service Desk and mark the ticket Security
  3. Notify Security CSIRT for confirmed or high-confidence events
  4. Do not delete emails or files that may be evidence

docs.title: Phishing reports
docs.text:
• Use the Report phishing button in Outlook
  • Forward as attachment only if the button is missing
  • Never enter credentials on linked pages from suspicious mail

docs.title: Service desk first response
docs.text: If credential theft is likely: reset password, revoke sessions, review MFA methods, then escalate to CSIRT with a timeline.

docs.title: Advanced Material showcase
docs.text:
What a high-end MkDocs site can look like

Not a tutorial — a live gallery of Material for MkDocs capabilities. Open this anytime from the ✦ star in the header.

docs.title: Rich callouts
docs.text:
Change window complete

Standard change CHG-10428 (print servers) closed with zero related incidents.

Elevated volume

Password-reset tickets +34% vs. weekly baseline after MFA re-enrollment campaign.

Do not share break-glass credentials

Emergency admin accounts require dual control and must be checked back into the vault within 60 minutes.

Deep dive — why this ticket spiked

A mobile OS update invalidated push MFA tokens for a subset of Android devices. Workaround: fall back to TOTP, then re-register the authenticator app.

docs.title: Multi-path procedures (tabs)
docs.text:
End userTier 1 agentAutomation
  1. Open password.contoso.example
  2. Complete MFA
  3. Set a 14+ character password
  4. Sign out of all apps and back in
  1. Verify identity (employee ID + manager + approved contact)
  2. Admin reset with force change at next sign-in
  3. Deliver temporary secret on a secure channel
  4. Document steps; resolve ticket
automation-hook.yml
trigger: ticket.category == "password-reset"
actions:
  - verify_identity:
      methods: [employee_id, manager_ok, mfa_device]
  - idp.reset_password:
      force_change: true
  - notify:
      channel: secure_message

docs.title: Intake & escalation
docs.text:
flowchart LR
  U[User] --> P[Portal / Chat / Phone]
  P --> T1[Tier 1]
  T1 -->|Known fix| R[Resolve + KB]
  T1 -->|Complex| T2[Tier 2]
  T2 --> T3[Tier 3 / Vendor]
  T1 -->|Sev 1| MI[Major incident]
  T2 --> MI
  MI --> R

docs.title: Agent assist sequence
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
  actor Agent
  participant KB as Knowledge base
  participant SM as Service mgmt
  participant User

  User->>SM: Open ticket
  Agent->>KB: Search symptom
  alt Article found
    KB-->>Agent: Procedure hit
    Agent->>User: Guided resolution
  else No article
    Agent->>SM: Escalate + flag KB gap
  end

docs.text:

docs.text:
journey
  title New hire IT day one
  section Arrive
    Collect laptop: 5: HR, Desk

  section Secure

docs.title: Command & config samples
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml (excerpt)PowerShell deployGitHub Actions idea 
theme:
  name: material
  features:
    - navigation.tabs
    - navigation.instant
    - search.suggest
    - content.code.annotate
    - content.tabs.link
markdown_extensions:
  - admonition
  - pymdownx.tabbed:
      alternate_style: true
  - pymdownx.superfences:
      custom_fences:
        - name: mermaid
          class: mermaid
          format: !!python/name:pymdownx.superfences.fence_code_format

.\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
mkdocs build
mkdocs gh-deploy --force

on:
  push:
    branches: [master]
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"
      - run: pip install -r requirements.txt
      - run: mkdocs gh-deploy --force

docs.title: Cards, buttons, and iconography
docs.text:
• Ship docs like product

    Versioned Markdown, PR review, instant preview, static publish

  • Search-first UX

    Suggestions, highlighting, shareable deep links into hits

  • Theme aware

    Light / dark / system — diagrams and chrome follow the palette

  • Visual systems thinking

    Mermaid flows beat five-page prose for escalations

Open overview Security quick ref GitHub repo

docs.title: Checklists & definitions
docs.text:
• Sticky navigation tabs
  • Instant loading + search suggest
  • Custom header action (✦ showcase)
  • Mermaid + annotated code
  • Optional: blog plugin, versioning, i18n
Incident Unplanned service interruption or degradation. Request Planned fulfillment work (access, hardware, software). Problem Underlying cause tracked across related incidents.

docs.title: Math & footnotes
docs.text:
Availability:

\[ A = \frac{\text{uptime}}{\text{uptime} + \text{downtime}} \]

Inline form \(A \ge 0.999\) for core collaboration services1.

docs.title: Quote strip
docs.text:
Great internal docs feel like a product: fast search, obvious structure, and visuals that remove ambiguity.

— What this showcase is meant to prove

  1. See SLA overview for exclusions and maintenance windows. ↩
